name: Potato Crepes
recipeIngredient: Russet potato, boiled in its skin until tender
recipeInstructions:
This elegant "Gourmet"-style recipe can also be found in "Cooking". It's a great example of "California Cuisine", combining non-Oriental ingredients with Chinese and European techniques. The potato crepes are fancy potato pancakes, while the seafood is prepared by poaching in low-temperature oil and then stir-frying - a classic Chinese technique. According to the author, Jay Harlow, this method involves cooking the seafood first with a light deep-frying, which most of the oil drains away, allowing it to be reused for other frying. The remaining oil in the dish is minimal compared to other stir-fried dishes.
1. Peel the potato and process it through a food mill or ricer.
2. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
3. Stir in the flour.
4. Beat the egg and milk together, then add to the potato mixture and stir until smooth.
5. Heat half the oil in a 10-inch nonstick skillet over medium heat.
6. Pour in half the potato mixture, shaping it into a thick pancake, about 8 to 9 inches in diameter. Cook until well-browned, 2 to 3 minutes, then turn and cook on the other side.
7. Transfer to a plate and keep warm in the oven. Repeat with the remaining oil and batter.
8. Makes 2 crepes.
